title: Betrayal at House on the Hill (3rd Edition)

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Character Materials
- **6** double-sided character sheets
- **6** character miniatures
- **6** miniature bases
- **4 per player** trait sliders

#### Books & References
- **2** haunt books (Secrets of Survival, Traitor’s Tome)
- **5** player reference cards
- **1** traitor reference card
- **1** monster reference card

#### Cards
- **74** game cards total (Event, Item, Omen)
- **5** scenario cards

#### Tiles & Tokens
- **3** starting tiles (ground-floor triple start tile, Basement Landing, Upper Landing)
- **1 stack** of remaining room tiles
- **114** cardboard tokens
- **1** tracker with indicator

#### Dice
- **2** dice

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Set books aside** — Keep both haunt books out of play until the haunt begins.
2. **Place start tiles** — Put the 3 starting tiles in the center area, spaced about 20–25 cm apart.
3. **Prepare room stack** — Shuffle non-start room tiles and place face down.
4. **Prepare card decks** — Separate Event, Item, and Omen cards; shuffle each deck and place face down.
5. **Place references** — Put traitor reference and monster reference face up for all players.
6. **Stage later-use pieces** — Keep tracker, indicator, and cardboard tokens nearby for haunt use.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Character Choice
- **Pick explorer** — Each player chooses one character.
- **Take matching pieces** — Take matching sheet, miniature, and color base.
- **Assemble mini** — Insert miniature into base.

#### Trait Setup
- **Take sliders** — Each player takes four sliders.
- **Set starting stats** — Place sliders on green starting values for Might, Speed, Knowledge, and Sanity.

#### Reference & Position
- **Reference card** — Each player takes one player reference card.
- **Starting room** — Place all explorers on Entrance Hall of the large start tile.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 — Player-Count Audit (From Provided Rulebook Excerpt)
- **Supported player counts** — The excerpt explicitly states **3–6 players**.
- **Found phrase** — The table of contents includes **“Ajustement au nombre de joueurs”** (adjustment by player count), but no exact numeric adjustment details are included in the provided text block.
- **No explicit setup split shown** — The provided setup excerpt does not give separate component counts or setup steps for 3, 4, 5, or 6 players.
- **No solo mode shown** — The provided excerpt contains no solo/solitaire setup section.

#### Scenario Start
- **Choose scenario** — Group picks 1 of 5 scenario cards.
- **Return unused cards** — Put remaining scenario cards back in box.
- **Scenario role** — Chosen scenario defines motivation and haunt branch for this game.

#### First Player
- **Birthday rule** — First player is the explorer whose birthday comes next.
- **Turn direction** — Play proceeds clockwise.

--- 3–6 Players ---
- **Team state** — Everyone begins on the same side in the pre-haunt phase.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Begin turn** — Start with current Speed and any active effects.
2. **Move** — Move through connected discovered rooms.
3. **Explore if able** — If entering unknown edge, reveal and place one new room tile.
4. **Resolve room/card** — Resolve room text and draw matching Event/Item/Omen if shown.
5. **Check haunt trigger** — If an Omen is revealed, perform haunt roll procedure.
6. **End turn** — In 3rd Edition, your turn usually ends when you discover and resolve a new room tile.

#### Haunt Roll Timing
- **Roll basis** — Roll one die per discovered Omen.
- **Trigger threshold** — Haunt starts on a qualifying result (5+ in the provided summary).
- **Fail-safe trigger** — If haunt still has not started by last Omen, haunt starts automatically.

#### Post-Haunt Turning Point
- **Mode shift** — After haunt starts, follow haunt-specific team rules and objectives.
- **Override rule** — Haunt text overrides core flow where specified.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Movement & Exploration
- **Move action** — Move through connected rooms up to legal movement.
- **Forced movement** — Card or room effects can force movement choices.
- **Discover room** — Revealing a new room expands the house layout uniquely each game.

#### Card Resolution
- **Event cards** — Usually one-time effects, often risky or swingy.
- **Item cards** — Usually grant persistent abilities or tactical tools.
- **Omen cards** — Powerful but dangerous; also affect haunt timing.

#### Checks & Traits
- **Trait checks** — Use Might, Speed, Knowledge, or Sanity as instructed.
- **Damage handling** — Move trait sliders down when harmed.
- **Healing handling** — Move sliders up when healed.
- **Critical loss** — If a critical trait reaches fatal level, explorer dies.

#### Interaction Options
- **Trading** — Exchange permitted cards/items when legal conditions are met.
- **Special actions** — Use explicit actions granted by cards or haunt text.

#### Haunt-Era Transparency Rule
- **Open action text** — If you use a haunt-book action, opponents may require full text read-aloud.
- **Secret reduction** — Once used, that action’s text is no longer private information.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Two-Phase Structure
- **Phase 1** — Cooperative exploration before the haunt.
- **Phase 2** — Haunt mode with split objectives and possible betrayal.

#### Haunt Composition
- **Possible formats** — Single traitor, hidden traitor, no traitor, or free-for-all structures (as referenced in the rules overview).
- **Scenario linkage** — Scenario card determines which haunt branch is used.

#### 3rd Edition Rules Changes Highlighted in Excerpt
- **Haunt roll change** — One die per discovered Omen; automatic trigger safeguard at final Omen.
- **Turn-end change** — Discovering/resolving a new room generally ends your turn.
- **No default stealing** — Item stealing is not a baseline combat reward unless haunt says so.
- **Corpse presence** — Dead explorers leave bodies on death tile.
- **Corpse looting** — Other explorers can loot items/omens from bodies.
- **Corpse relevance** — Some haunts use corpses as objective elements.

#### Information Model During Haunt
- **Action disclosure** — Performed haunt actions can be requested and read aloud.
- **Priority rule** — If text conflicts, haunt text governs.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition Type
- **No VP system** — Betrayal 3e does not use universal victory points.
- **Objective win** — Game ends immediately when a side completes its haunt objective.

#### Haunt Resolution Flow
1. **Haunt begins** — Teams are determined by haunt/scenario instructions.
2. **Read intros aloud** — Both sides read setup/introduction text before splitting.
3. **Execute objectives** — Heroes and traitor side pursue separate win conditions.
4. **Immediate end** — First side to satisfy objective wins instantly.

#### Tie-Break / Ambiguity Handling
- **Primary authority** — Haunt text resolves edge cases first.
- **Fallback** — Use group consensus if unresolved, as recommended by the excerpt’s “keep playing” guidance.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Explore with spacing discipline** — Spread enough to reveal rooms, but keep support distance so weak explorers are not stranded at haunt flip.
- **Distribute power cards** — Avoid stacking all strong items/omens on one explorer before role reveal.
- **Protect fragile stats** — Players with low Speed or Might should avoid deep isolation paths early.
- **Resolve every card carefully** — Small pre-haunt gains compound into post-haunt survival odds.
- **Track scenario context** — Treat early turns as setup for a likely team split, not pure long-form co-op.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Using old haunt rules** — Applying prior-edition haunt logic breaks 3e timing and balance.
- **Taking illegal extra actions** — Forgetting that room discovery typically ends turn in 3e.
- **Single-carrier collapse** — Giving all key gear to one explorer can lose the game if they die or flip sides.
- **Slow role transition** — Continuing cooperative behavior after objectives split.
- **Skipping haunt setup lines** — Missing one setup instruction often decides the game.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Speed vs safety** — Faster exploration increases card gain but accelerates omen pressure toward haunt.
- **Group vs split** — Grouping improves protection/trading, splitting improves map reveal tempo.
- **Concentration vs resilience** — Focused item stacks create spikes; distributed tools reduce betrayal/death blowouts.

metadata.notes: Primary source used: provided Betrayal at House on the Hill 3rd Edition rulebook excerpt (French). Reformatted for scanability with explicit player-count audit.
